name: Configuration Constants
description: Centralized configuration constants for database, cache, auth, realtime, storage, errors, and environment variables.

intent.what: Provides a single source of truth for all system constants including pagination limits, cache TTLs, auth settings, realtime timeouts, storage limits, error codes, and environment variable keys.
intent.why: Ensures consistency across the codebase, enables easy configuration changes, and prevents hardcoded magic numbers scattered throughout the system.

inputs.name: constantGroup
inputs.description: Constant group name (DATABASE, CACHE, AUTH, REALTIME, STORAGE, ERRORS, ENV_KEYS)

outputs.description: Constant values for the specified group

steps: Reference constants in configuration, validation, and error handling
Override environment-specific values via environment variables

logic.summary: Constants are organized into logical groups (DATABASE, CACHE, AUTH, REALTIME, STORAGE, ERRORS, ENV_KEYS) and exported from a single file. Values are typed and documented to prevent misuse. Environment-specific values are read from environment variables at runtime.

flow.detail: Import specific group or entire constants object
Reference constants instead of magic numbers
Some values can be overridden via environment variables

rules: Constants must be immutable at runtime
Do not modify constants directly; use environment variables for configuration
All constants must be documented with their purpose and unit (ms, bytes, etc.)
New constants must follow existing naming conventions (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE)
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode values that are already defined in constants
Do not create duplicate constants with different names
Do not modify constants at runtime
Do not add domain-specific constants to core groups

constants.name: DATABASE.RETRY_DELAY
constants.description: Base delay between retries in milliseconds

constants.name: CACHE.DEFAULT_TTL
constants.description: Default cache TTL in milliseconds (5 minutes)

constants.name: CACHE.KEY_SEPARATOR
constants.description: Separator used in cache key generation

constants.name: AUTH.SESSION_KEY
constants.description: Local storage key for session token

constants.name: ENV_KEYS.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
constants.description: Environment variable for Supabase anon key

constants.name: ENV_KEYS.SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY
constants.description: Environment variable for service role key

when_to_use: When configuring pagination, timeouts, or retry settings
When setting cache TTL or size limits
When validating file sizes or types
When handling specific error types by code
When reading environment variables for Supabase configuration
when_not_to_use: When domain-specific constants are needed (create separate config)
When runtime configuration is required (use environment variables)
When constants need to be user-configurable (use settings system)
reasoning_steps: Identify which constant group contains the needed value
Use the constant instead of hardcoding the value
Check if environment variable override is available
Ensure constant is appropriate for the use case (e.g., MAX_PAGE_SIZE vs DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE)
